headline: Mongol Empire
description: The Mongol Empire (1206-1368) was the largest contiguous land empire in history (24 million km² at its peak). Founded by Genghis Khan (Temujin) who united Mongol tribes. Mongols were fearsome warriors but also facilitated trade, cultural exchange, and religious tolerance across Eurasia (Pax Mongolica).

citation.name: Genghis Khan and the Making of the Modern World (Jack Weatherford)
